steps.title: Spot the singular subject
steps.content: The sentence opens with a prepositional phrase (“Among the clubs' leaders”), but the true subject is the noun phrase that follows: “Josephine St. Pierre Ruffin, founder of the Women's Era Club of Boston.” This subject is singular—one person.

steps.title: Apply subject-verb agreement
steps.content: Subject-verb agreement requires verbs to match their subjects in number. The singular subject requires “was”, the singular verb form. “Were,” “are,” and “have been” are all plural forms that violate this agreement requirement. That matches choice A — A is correct.
